2604.24525.title: Understanding the Limits of Automated Evaluation for Code Review Bots in Practice
2604.24525.paper_summary: Automated code review (ACR) bots are increasingly used in industrial software development to assist developers during pull request (PR) review. As adoption grows, a key challenge is how to evaluate the usefulness of bot-generated comments reliably and at scale. In practice, such evaluation often relies on developer actions and annotations that are shaped by contextual and organizational factors, complicating their use as objective ground truth. We examine the feasibility and limitations of automating the evaluation of LLM-powered ACR bots in an industrial setting. We analyze an industrial dataset from Beko comprising 2,604 bot-generated PR comments, each labeled by software engineers as fixed/wontFix. Two automated evaluation approaches, G-Eval and an LLM-as-a-Judge pipeline, are applied using both binary decisions and a 0-4 Likert-scale formulation, enabling a controlled comparison against developer-provided labels. Across Gemini-2.5-pro, GPT-4.1-mini, and GPT-5.2, both evaluation strategies achieve only moderate alignment with human labels. Agreement ratios range from approximately 0.44 to 0.62, with noticeable variation across models and between binary and Likert-scale formulations, indicating sensitivity to both model choice and evaluation design. Our findings highlight practical limitations in fully automating the evaluation of ACR bot comments in industrial contexts. Developer actions such as resolving or ignoring comments reflect not only comment quality, but also contextual constraints, prioritization decisions, and workflow dynamics that are difficult to capture through static artifacts. Insights from a follow-up interview with a software engineering director further corroborate that developer labeling behavior is strongly influenced by workflow pressures and organizational constraints, reinforcing the challenges of treating such signals as objective ground truth.
2604.24525.paper_summary_zh: 自動化代碼審查（ACR）機器人越來越多地用於工業軟體開發，以協助開發人員在拉取請求（PR）審查期間。隨著採用率的增加，一個關鍵挑戰是如何可靠且大規模地評估機器人生成的評論的有用性。實際上，這種評估通常依賴於開發人員的行動和註釋，這些行動和註釋受到上下文和組織因素的影響，使其難以作為客觀的真實依據。我們檢視在工業環境中自動化評估基於LLM的ACR機器人的可行性和限制。我們分析了來自Beko的工業數據集，其中包含2,604條機器人生成的PR評論，每條評論均由軟體工程師標記為fixed/wontFix。兩種自動化評估方法，G-Eval和LLM-as-a-Judge管道，使用二元決策和0-4李克特量表的形式進行應用，從而實現與開發人員提供的標籤之間的受控比較。在Gemini-2.5-pro、GPT-4.1-mini和GPT-5.2中，這兩種評估策略僅與人類標籤達成中等一致性。協議比率範圍約為0.44到0.62，模型之間以及二元和李克特量表形式之間存在明顯的變化，顯示出對模型選擇和評估設計的敏感性。我們的研究結果突顯了在工業環境中完全自動化評估ACR機器人評論的實際限制。開發人員的行動，例如解決或忽略評論，不僅反映了評論質量，還反映了上下文限制、優先決策和工作流程動態，這些都是難以通過靜態文檔捕捉的。來自與一位軟體工程總監的後續訪談的見解進一步證實，開發人員的標記行為受到工作流程壓力和組織限制的強烈影響，這加強了將這些信號視為客觀真實依據的挑戰。
2604.24525.author: Veli Karakaya et.al.
2604.24525.authors: Veli Karakaya, Utku Boran Torun, Baykal Mehmet Uçar, Eray Tüzün
